name: Possible Persistence via Mandatory User Profile File
description: An NTUSER.MAN file was created within a user profile directory. This file type represents a mandatory user profile that takes precedence over NTUSER.DAT during logon. Attackers can abuse this mechanism to achieve persistence by placing a malicious NTUSER.MAN file that loads attacker-controlled registry settings whenever the user logs in.

filterSets.name: Possible Persistence via Mandatory User Profile File

author: Peligey Rufus & Yusuf Madkour
